rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic intramolecular cyclopropanation of active methylene cyanoamides bearing tethered alkenes using a phenothiazine organocatalyst (PT-1) in TFE to give cyclopropane-fused quinolinones and pyrrolidinones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of phenothiazine catalyst PT-1 to its persistent radical cation [PT-1]•+, which serves as the active oxidant; cathodic reduction of CF3CH2OH generates CF3CH2O- (base) and H2.

key_intermediates.name: phenothiazine radical cation [PT-1]•+ (SOMO with significant S character)
deprotonated cyanoamide anion (70 -> anion after CF3CH2O- deprotonation)
alpha-cyano carbon radical 71 (electrophilic C-radical on cyanoamide)
cyclized alkyl radical 72 (after 5-exo/exo-trig radical cyclization onto pendant alkene)
sulfonium adduct 73 (C-S bond between alkyl radical 72 and [PT-1]•+)
substrate-catalyst adduct 68 (analog of 73 from methylated substrate 67) detected by HRMS at m/z 608.2032
cyclopropane product 74 + regenerated PT-1 (after SN2-like displacement by CF3CH2O-)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PT-1 to [PT-1]•+; cathodically generated CF3CH2O- deprotonates the active methylene substrate, and [PT-1]•+ oxidizes the resulting carbanion to an alpha-cyano C-radical, which undergoes exo-trig cyclization; the cyclized alkyl radical couples with another [PT-1]•+ to give a sulfonium intermediate that is opened by CF3CH2O- in a Corey-Chaykovsky-like SN2 to deliver the cyclopropane and regenerate PT-1.

probe_reagents.name: cis/trans-alkene stereoisomers 64 and 65
probe_reagents.observation: Both isomers gave similar yields and a mixture of diastereomers (1.7:1 and 1.3:1 dr), indicating loss of alkene stereochemistry and a stepwise radical cyclization rather than concerted carbene-like addition.

probe_reagents.name: alpha-methylated cyanoamide 67
probe_reagents.observation: Blocks the cyclization step after C-S bond formation; substrate-catalyst sulfonium adduct 68 detected by HRMS (608.2032, calc 608.2036), supporting sulfonium intermediate 73.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1, PT-1, and PT-1 + 1 + LiOMe
probe_reagents.observation: Catalytic current observed only with both amide 1 and base present, showing base-assisted electrocatalytic oxidation of substrate by [PT-1]•+.

probe_reagents.name: DFT calculation of [PT-1]•+ SOMO and exo- vs endo-trig cyclization
probe_reagents.observation: SOMO is distributed across the tricyclic ring with significant density on sulfur (rationalizing C-S coupling); exo-trig cyclization is kinetically favored over endo even for 1,1-disubstituted alkenes.

notes: Authors emphasize that efficient radical cyclization of 71 is essential — otherwise the open-chain alpha-cyano radical can couple with [PT-1]•+ to form undesired sulfur ylides. The C-S/SN2 termination is explicitly analogized to the Corey-Chaykovsky cyclopropanation. The corresponding adduct 69 from non-methylated substrate 1 was not observed by HRMS, consistent with fast turnover. No exogenous base is needed because cathodic reduction of TFE continuously generates CF3CH2O-.
